claim: Qwen3-TTS initial open-source release achieved 0.8x real-time (1 second of audio took 1.2 seconds); optimization to static KV cache + CUDA graph captures brought it to 5.8x real-time with <200ms first-token latency
actionable: For TTS systems, replace dynamic KV caches with static ones to enable CUDA graph compilation, dramatically reducing latency for voice agents.
source_title: Reachy Mini: the $300 open source robot you can actually hack — Andres Marafioti, Hugging Face

claim: TTS models bottleneck on CPU-GPU round trips: 500 autoregressive steps per audio packet require coordination between CPU and GPU; streaming generation and CUDA compilation solve this
actionable: Implement streaming TTS and compile models to GPU to avoid CPU-GPU synchronization overhead on each generation step.
source_title: Reachy Mini: the $300 open source robot you can actually hack — Andres Marafioti, Hugging Face

claim: Voice-interactive robots are becoming viable at low cost ($300 for Reachy Mini) with open-source models; talking to the robot is the most-used app by far across 7,500+ units shipped
actionable: Invest in voice agent capabilities for robotics products—user engagement data shows voice interaction drives adoption far more than other modalities.
source_title: Reachy Mini: the $300 open source robot you can actually hack — Andres Marafioti, Hugging Face

claim: Parakeet transcription runs every 150 milliseconds with partial results fed back to the robot mid-sentence, enabling real-time conversational feedback
actionable: Implement streaming speech-to-text with partial result feedback to create responsive voice interactions without waiting for full transcription.
source_title: Reachy Mini: the $300 open source robot you can actually hack — Andres Marafioti, Hugging Face

claim: Infrastructure round-trip latency often matches or exceeds model latency in voice agents—separate LLM endpoints from conversation nodes to handle different talk-frequency patterns across users
actionable: Scale LLM and conversation nodes independently based on usage patterns—heavy talkers vs. light talkers have very different resource requirements.
source_title: Reachy Mini: the $300 open source robot you can actually hack — Andres Marafioti, Hugging Face

claim: Dynamic KV cache prevents model compilation to GPU; static KV cache trades memory for speed, enabling CUDA graph captures that dramatically accelerate generation
actionable: Use static KV cache allocations in TTS/streaming models to allow GPU compilation and graph capture optimizations.
source_title: Reachy Mini: the $300 open source robot you can actually hack — Andres Marafioti, Hugging Face

claim: Open-source robotics enables hackability and custom interactions that proprietary systems prevent—users can repurpose Reachy Mini with 3D-printed parts, triggering creative experimentation
actionable: Design AI products with open-source foundations and extensibility to unlock user creativity and organic use cases.
source_title: Reachy Mini: the $300 open source robot you can actually hack — Andres Marafioti, Hugging Face
